title: Centers of a Tree (Minimum Height Trees)
description:
Given an undirected tree with n nodes labeled 0..n-1, return all the centers that yield minimum height when chosen as roots. Remove leaves layer by layer to find the 1 or 2 centers.

    

Constraints:
    - 1 ≤ n ≤ 2·10^4
- Edges length n-1
explanation:
Intuition
The center(s) of a tree are found by trimming leaves iteratively until 1 or 2 nodes remain. This simulates reversing a BFS from the leaves.

Approach
1. Initial Setup: Build degree array and adjacency
2. Main Algorithm: Initialize queue with all leaves (deg=1); pop layers and decrement neighbors
3. Return Result: Return remaining 1 or 2 nodes

Complexity Analysis
- Time Complexity: O(n)
- Space Complexity: O(n)

Key Insights
- At most two centers exist
- Process nodes layer by layer

Common Pitfalls
- For n=1 return [0]
- Do not drop nodes of degree 0 except at the end
